title: Allergy list update
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.
assessment.rationale: The exact structured-documentation section supports recording a suspected or confirmed drug name, reaction signs and symptoms, severity, date, timing, indication, doses before onset, route, status, and specialist results without confirming allergy.

source.title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, emergency, medicine, and referral adaptation
source.population: People of all ages with a possible or established drug allergy or suspected drug-related reaction.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, inpatient, outpatient, and specialist-allergy documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting suspected-drug identity, indication, dose-count or exposure duration, route, reaction description, severity, timing, examination, and structured allergy record. It does not diagnose drug allergy, stop a medicine, generate treatment, or make a referral.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.9 — recording and sharing drug-allergy information

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.

assessment.text: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.
assessment.rationale: The exact medicines-communication section supports clinician-entered reconciliation, communication, patient questions, safety-net, and follow-up documentation without changing the allergy list automatically.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, and young people using medicines, with specific transfer, reconciliation, and decision-involvement recommendations.
source.setting: Transfers between care settings and clinician-led medicines consultations.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting current medicines, changes made during transfer, reconciliation, discrepancies, person or carer involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-led decisions. It does not generate a medicine start, stop, dose, prescription, or deprescribing decision.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.
